draft.introduction: The longest chapter in the Bible, an acrostic meditation on Torah. This first section (Aleph) establishes the theme of wholehearted delight in God’s law.

translation.text: Blessed are those whose way is blameless, who walk in the law of the Lord! Blessed are those who keep his testimonies, who seek him with their whole heart. With my whole heart I seek you; let me not wander from your commandments!
translation.original_text: μακάριοι οἱ ἄμωμοι ἐν ὁδῷ οἱ πορευόμενοι ἐν νόμῳ κυρίου

translator_notes.note: The LXX ἄμωμοι (“blameless”) translates Hebrew tamim, the same word used of Noah (Gen 6:9) and Abraham (Gen 17:1), connecting Torah observance to the moral perfection God commands.
